round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent effectively integrated landscape and architectural evidence to rule out other Lusophone countries, strengthening the Brazil conclusion.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Agent incorporated linguistic evidence ('temos frango') to confirm the location, moving beyond just visual landscape cues.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Agent used the linguistic evidence to rule out Spanish-speaking tropical countries (Colombia/Venezuela), refining the probability.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Agent synthesized linguistic and architectural evidence to support the regulatory assessment, though the regulatory evidence itself remained weak.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Agent successfully cross-referenced architectural details with linguistic and landscape findings to reinforce the Brazil hypothesis.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly identified the convergence of evidence across all agents. The synthesis explicitly linked the Portuguese text ('temos frango') with the red latosol soil and specific architectural features (clay tiles, security grilles) to definitively rule out other Lusophone or tropical regions. The reasoning is sound and fully supported by the image.
